previous_roles: Regional CISO UK & Ireland, Deutsche Bank
Head of Group Information Security Office, Deutsche Bank (2008–2016)
education: Diploma in Computer Science, Karlsruhe Institute of Technology
Doctorate in Business Computer Science, Justus Liebig University Giessen

items.title: First-quarter results 2026 — restructuring charge €190M
items.summary: Q1 2026 income €2.9bn, EPS €0.36, ROE 15.4%. €190M restructuring charge to deliver ≥€150M annual cost reduction by 2028.

items.title: Nordea to book restructuring costs to execute its 2030 strategy
items.summary: 2030 strategy: 6 growth areas, unified Nordic corporate credit platform, scalable payments platform. Tech/data/AI central.

items.title: Detection, prevention, response — staying ahead of cyber threats
items.summary: Multi-layered cybersecurity strategy: 5 pillars (identity, protect, detect, respond, recover). Access management = backbone.

items.title: Beyond a bank: deep dive into Nordea's tech landscape
items.summary: Tech transformation profile of Nordea internal systems.

findings.summary: DORA enforcement is live in 2026. Article 28 requires ICT third-party risk management — covers mobile-device vendors. Mobile security needs go beyond basic MDMs (Intune).
findings.implication_for_sale: HMD Secure can position as DORA-compliant alternative — EU manufacturing + audit trail + sovereign data residency.

findings.summary: Massive cost-out program (€150M+ annual). Workforce composition changing. Tech consolidation across Nordics.
findings.implication_for_sale: Procurement consolidation moment — vendors being re-evaluated. Window of opportunity to displace incumbents.

findings.summary: Nordea's 5-pillar framework (identity, protect, detect, respond, recover). Marc has openly spoken about regulation driving board accountability.
findings.implication_for_sale: Aligns with HMD Secure's end-to-end European chain — argument lands at executive level.

findings.summary: Spoke at 2024 Nordic CISO Executive Summit on regulation driving board accountability. KuppingerCole speaker. Sibos TV 2018 interview.
findings.implication_for_sale: Opens conversation: cite his Nordic CISO Summit theme of board accountability — DORA is the live test.

findings.summary: Ex-SWIFT CISO. ENISA Advisory Group. PhD. Deep regulatory + crypto/payments background.
findings.implication_for_sale: Don't oversell basics — he knows them. Bring up advanced topics: supply-chain attacks, post-quantum readiness, hardware roots of trust.

findings.summary: LinkedIn = Denmark-based. Many roles UK/Germany. Nordea group role, not Finland-only.
findings.implication_for_sale: Speaks English, not Finnish. Reference DK/UK/DE customer cases, not only Finnish ones.

pre_meeting_brief.headline: Marc Hofmann, Group CSO Nordea since 2019. Ex-SWIFT CISO + ENISA. PhD in Business Computer Science. Cybersecurity is board-level priority during Nordea's 2030 restructuring (€190M charge Q1 2026).
pre_meeting_brief.rapport_opener: Saw your Nordic CISO Summit talk on regulation driving board accountability — that's exactly the lens DORA Article 28 is pushing on mobile-device vendors right now.
three_discovery_questions: Where does mobile fleet management sit within the 2030 restructuring — consolidating across the Nordics, or staying per-country?
How is Nordea approaching DORA Article 28 ICT third-party risk for mobile-device vendors specifically — are you re-papering existing contracts or restarting the RFP?
Given your SWIFT background, how comparable is the security posture you want for Nordea's mobile workflows vs SWIFT-connected operations?
pre_meeting_brief.product_interest_flag: Lead selected HMD Fota, but Marc's role (CSO) and Nordea's situation (DORA + 2030 consolidation) point to HMD Enable Pro as the strategic conversation. Fota is a feature of Enable Pro — open with broader EMM scope, then mention Fota as one capability.
competitor_intel: Nordea likely runs Intune today (Microsoft shop). Intune is explicitly called out in DORA-readiness articles as insufficient for finance.
Watch for: Samsung Knox-Suite, BlackBerry UEM, Hexnode.
things_not_to_mention: Generic 'security' pitch — he's heard them all. Go specific.
Don't oversell Finnish manufacturing as nostalgia — Marc is DK/UK/DE background. Frame it as EU sovereignty + supply-chain integrity, not heritage.
pre_meeting_brief.decision_chain: Marc Hofmann (CSO) → likely CIO + Procurement → Group CFO sign-off on multi-year contract. Nordea's restructuring window suggests CFO is unusually involved.
pre_meeting_brief.deal_sizing: Nordea ~30,000 employees. Mobile fleet realistic 8,000–15,000 devices (relationship managers, branch staff, executives). Multi-year fixed-term contract with FOTA + EMM. Estimated 3-yr TCV €3–6M.
